name: Widow Fairs

proposed.name: Widow Fairs
proposed.identity_line: Mass matchmaking of northern soldiers with riverlands widows at multiple locations

edges.quote: more than a thousand northmen accompanied Black Aly and her nephew Lord Benjicot when they returned to the riverlands after the royal wedding

notes: Quote verified at line 23. Multiple locations mentioned (Raventree, Riverrun, Stoney Sept, Twins, Fairmarket). Lady Alysanne Targaryen credited as originator.

name: Wedding of Aegon III and Jaehaera

proposed.name: Wedding of Aegon III and Jaehaera
proposed.identity_line: Wedding of king Aegon III and princess Jaehaera at Visenya's Hill, 131 AC

edges.quote: the wedding of Prince Aegon and Princess Jaehaera was celebrated out of doors, at the top of Visenya's Hill
the wedding of Prince Aegon and Princess Jaehaera was celebrated out of doors, at the top of Visenya's Hill

notes: Quote verified at line 51. Solemnized on seventh day of seventh moon 131 AC. High Septon of Oldtown performed rites. Aegon crowned afterward with yellow gold circlet.

name: Velaryon succession attack on Alyn

proposed.name: Velaryon Succession Attack on Alyn
proposed.identity_line: Failed assassination plot against Lord Alyn by rival Velaryon claimants

edges.quote: However, the plot to murder their young lord went awry when the guards at Castle Driftmark proved loyal to the Sea Snake's memory and his chosen heir
Ser Malentine was slain during the attempt

notes: Quote verified at line 167. The unnamed Velaryon silent cousins (Daemion and Daeron also tried). Ser Malentine slain, his brother captured and condemned to death; brother (Ser Rhogar) took the black instead.

name: Death of Alicent Hightower

proposed.name: Death of Alicent Hightower
proposed.identity_line: Death of Dowager Queen Alicent from Winter Fever, same night as Lord Westerling

edges.quote: The Dowager Queen Alicent of House Hightower, second wife of King Viserys | and mother to his sons, Aegon, Aemond, and Daeron, and his daughter Helaena, died on the same night as Lord Westerling

notes: Quote verified at line 319. Occurred during 132 AC Winter Fever. Alicent had confined herself to apartments, unraveled mentally ('deep aversion to color green'), spoke of seeing her dead children. Died at hour of the wolf (midnight).

name: Aemond named Prince Regent

proposed.name: Aemond Named Prince Regent
proposed.identity_line: Prince Aemond assumes regency after Aegon II recovers from Meleys burn

edges.quote: named himself only Protector of the Realm and Prince Regent

notes: Quote verified at line 153 with expanded context from line 154. Aemond claimed iron-and-ruby crown but styled himself Protector/Regent, not King. Criston Cole remained Hand.

name: Legitimization of Addam and Alyn

proposed.name: Legitimization of Addam and Alyn
proposed.identity_line: Queen Rhaenyra removes bastardy taint from dragonseed brothers Addam and Alyn of Hull

edges.quote: Addam of Hull, dragonseed and bastard, became Addam Velaryon
Addam of Hull, dragonseed and bastard, became Addam Velaryon

notes: Quote spans lines 235-237. Petition originated from Lord Corlys and was seconded by Prince Jacaerys. Addam thereafter styled heir to Driftmark.

name: Taming of Sheepstealer by Nettles

proposed.name: Taming of Sheepstealer by Nettles
proposed.identity_line: Nettles becomes rider of wild dragon Sheepstealer via patient daily sheep offerings

edges.quote: the brown dragon was brought to heel by the cunning and persistence of a "small brown girl" of six-and-ten

notes: Quote verified at line 249. Nettles was 16 years old, delivered fresh sheep every morning until Sheepstealer accepted her. Multiple accounts of her birth (Munkun=bastard of unknown origin; Mushroom=dockside whore's daughter).

name: Citadel sends white ravens

proposed.name: Citadel Sends White Ravens (130 AC)
proposed.identity_line: Citadel announces approaching winter via three hundred white ravens sent on Maiden's Day

edges.quote: On Maiden's Day in the year 130 AC, the Citadel of Oldtown sent forth three hundred white ravens to herald the coming of winter

notes: Quote verified at line 149-150 in fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p03. Despite the warning of winter, conditions favored Rhaenyra's side militarily (Kingslanders disaffected, Velaryon fleet strong, Vale loyal, North sending aid).

name: Rising of the Shepherd

proposed.name: Rising of the Shepherd
proposed.identity_line: One-handed itinerant preacher stirs masses in Cobbler's Square with anti-dragon sermons during Rhaenyra's reign

edges.quote: there rose up in Cobbler's Square a certain itinerant brother, a barefoot scarecrow of a man ina hair shirt and roughspun breeches, filthy and unwashed and smelling of the sty

notes: Quote verified at line 123 in fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p04. Shepherd was likely a Poor Fellow, had lost right hand, preached dragons as Valyrian demons. Crowd grew to thousands; gold cloaks driven off attempting to suppress.

name: Gunthor slays Alester Wynch

proposed.name: Gunthor Slays Alester Wynch
proposed.identity_line: Gunthor Goodbrother kills commander Alester Wynch in dispute over Lysa Farman

edges.quote: the latter slew the former in a quarrel over Lord Farman's daughter Lysa
the latter slew the former in a quarrel over Lord Farman's daughter Lysa

notes: Quote verified at line 99. Context: Faircastle garrison holding out against Fair Isle rebellion. Lysa was one of Dalton Greyjoy's salt wives. Afterward, longships fled and Fair Isle freed.

name: Birth of Laena Velaryon

proposed.name: Birth of Laena Velaryon (daughter of Alyn)
proposed.identity_line: Baela Targaryen gives birth to daughter Laena while Alyn Velaryon sails to Lys, 134 AC

edges.quote: Baela Velaryon was brought to childbed and delivered of a healthy baby girl

notes: Quote verified at line 139. Occurs while Alyn is away; named after her mother (Alyn's deceased wife). She is daughter of Alyn Velaryon and Baela Targaryen (also known as Baela Velaryon). Node laena-velaryon-daughter-of-alyn already exists in graph.

name: Ransom pact for Prince Viserys

proposed.name: Ransom Pact for Prince Viserys
proposed.identity_line: Alyn Velaryon agrees to ruinous terms with Lysene magister to ransom missing Prince Viserys

edges.quote: the Iron Throne would pay a ransom of one hundred thousand golden dragons
the Iron Throne would pay a ransom of one hundred thousand golden dragons

notes: Quote verified at line 183. Terms included: 100k dragons ransom, non-aggression vs House Rogare for 100 years, transfer Braavos Bank funds to Rogare Bank, lordships for three Rogare sons, marriage to Larra Rogare permanent. Prince Viserys returned 134 AC.

name: Death of Tyland Lannister

proposed.name: Death of Tyland Lannister
proposed.identity_line: Hand Tyland Lannister dies of Winter Fever in 132 AC, attended by king Aegon III

edges.quote: The fever took his life in two days instead of the usual four

notes: Quote verified at line 15. Died quickly, faster than typical Winter Fever. Aegon III held his hand as he died. Tyland had served both Rhaenyra and Aegon II/III, made no enemies on either side despite blindness and disfigurement from torture.

name: Death of Herman Rollingford

proposed.name: Death of Herman Rollingford
proposed.identity_line: Ser Herman Rollingford slain by Braavosi bravo in duel over doublet color during peace mission

edges.quote: Ser Herman Rollingford was killed in a duel by a bravo who took offense at the color of his doublet

notes: Quote verified at line 215. Occurred during Lord Mooton's peace mission to Braavos following Alyn Velaryon's conflict with the Sealord. Trivial cause of death (doublet color dispute).

name: Death of Aegon II

proposed.name: Death of Aegon II
proposed.identity_line: King Aegon II dies of poison in his litter en route to sept, suspected to be Larys Strong's behest

edges.quote: the king dead upon his cushions
we can have no doubt that it was done at the behest of Larys Strong

notes: Quote verified at line 277-278. Poisoner unknown (Ser Gyles or Ummet food-taster suspected). Aegon had been sickly, ruled only 2 years. Folding row 4 cremation detail: body was carried back to Dragonstone and cremated per Targaryen tradition (no cremation quote provided in input but reference to it in passage context).
